revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 画像=| 名称=貴船神社| 所在地=岐阜県大垣市本町1丁目75 | 祭神=[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] 家津御子命大神| 創建=明治4...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 画像=| 名称=貴船神社| 所在地=岐阜県大垣市本町1丁目75 | 祭神=[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] 家津御子命大神| 創建=明治4年| 神事=本町お火焚きまつり| 例祭=8月5日}}
'''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]本町にある[[神社]]。
大垣市本町の[[水門川]]沿いにある。
== 概略 == [[戸田氏鉄]]が[[摂津国]][[尼崎藩]]藩主の時、[[京都]]貴船神社から分社しし祀ったのが始まりという。戸田氏鉄が尼崎藩藩主であったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）から[[寛永]]12年（[[1635年]]）であることから、分社したのはこの時期と推測される。
寛永12年に[[美濃国]][[大垣藩]]に移封されたさい、この貴船神社も[[大垣城]]内に移転。[[戸田氏]]は貴船神社の他、[[熊野権現|熊野神社]]など7社を大垣城内に祀っていた。
[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）4月頃、大垣城下の本町の住民が旧藩主の[[戸田氏共]]に願い出、大垣城内の貴船神社と熊野神社を本町の鎮守として祀る。同年10月、遮那院（[[白鳳]]年間に[[百済]]の王子沙門金珠が創建したという寺。明治2年廃寺。現在の大垣市清水町付近）の東惣門櫓台跡地に貴船神社と熊野神社を合祀、貴船神社を祀る。
== 祭神 == *[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] *家津御子命大神
== 交通機関 == *[[東海道本線]]・[[養老鉄道]]・[[樽見鉄道]][[大垣駅]]より徒歩10分。
== その他 == *水門川沿いに水門川遊歩道四季の路が整備されている。隣接する貴船広場は、様々な[[イベント]]の会場となる他、[[イルミネーション]]が施される。 *毎年11月3日に行われる「本町お火焚きまつり」では、本町通りが[[歩行者天国]]となり、[[フリーマーケット]]などが行われる。
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしやおおかきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:大垣市]]

{{神社| 画像=| 名称=貴船神社| 所在地=岐阜県大垣市本町1丁目75 | 祭神=[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] 家津御子命大神| 創建=明治4年| 神事=本町お火焚きまつり| 例祭=8月5日}}
'''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]本町にある[[神社]]。
大垣市本町の[[水門川]]沿いにある。
== 概略 == [[戸田氏鉄]]が[[摂津国]][[尼崎藩]]藩主の時、[[京都]]の[[貴船神社]]から分社しし祀ったのが始まりという。戸田氏鉄が尼崎藩藩主であったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）から[[寛永]]12年（[[1635年]]）であることから、分社したのはこの時期と推測される。
寛永12年に[[美濃国]][[大垣藩]]に移封されたさい、この貴船神社も[[大垣城]]内に移転。[[戸田氏]]は貴船神社の他、[[熊野権現|熊野神社]]など7社を大垣城内に祀っていた。
[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）4月頃、大垣城下の本町の住民が旧藩主の[[戸田氏共]]に願い出、大垣城内の貴船神社と熊野神社を本町の鎮守として祀る。同年10月、遮那院（[[白鳳]]年間に[[百済]]の王子沙門金珠が創建したという寺。明治2年廃寺。現在の大垣市清水町付近）の東惣門櫓台跡地に貴船神社と熊野神社を合祀、貴船神社を祀る。
== 祭神 == *[[淤加美神|高靇大神]] *家津御子命大神
== 交通機関 == *[[東海道本線]]・[[養老鉄道]]・[[樽見鉄道]][[大垣駅]]より徒歩10分。
== その他 == *水門川沿いに水門川遊歩道四季の路が整備されている。隣接する貴船広場は、様々な[[イベント]]の会場となる他、[[イルミネーション]]が施される。 *毎年11月3日に行われる「本町お火焚きまつり」では、本町通りが[[歩行者天国]]となり、[[フリーマーケット]]などが行われる。
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしやおおかきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:大垣市]]

{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 岐阜県大垣市本町1丁目75 |位置 = |祭神 = [[淤加美神|高靇大神]] 家津御子命大神 |社格 = |創建 = 明治4年 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 8月5日 |神事 = 本町お火焚きまつり }}

== 交通機関 == *[[東海道本線]]・[[養老鉄道]]・[[樽見鉄道]][[大垣駅]]より徒歩10分。
== その他 == *水門川沿いに水門川遊歩道四季の路が整備されている。隣接する貴船広場は、様々な[[イベント]]の会場となる他、[[イルミネーション]]が施される。 *毎年11月3日に行われる「本町お火焚きまつり」では、本町通りが[[歩行者天国]]となり、[[フリーマーケット]]などが行われる。
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしやおおかきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:大垣市]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 岐阜県大垣市本町1丁目75 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[高龗神|高龗大神]]
[[家津御子命大神]] |社格 = |創建 = 江戸時代初め |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月5日 |神事 = 本町お火焚きまつり }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]本町にある[[神社]]。大垣市本町の[[水門川]]沿いにある。 == 祭神 == *[[高龗神|高龗大神]] *[[家津御子命大神]] == 由緒 == [[戸田氏鉄]]が[[摂津国]][[尼崎藩]]藩主の時、[[京都]]の[[貴船神社]]から分社して祀ったのが始まりという。戸田氏鉄が尼崎藩藩主であったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）から[[寛永]]12年（[[1635年]]）であることから、分社したのはこの時期と推測される。 [[寛永]]12年（1635年）に[[美濃国]][[大垣藩]]に移封された際、当神社も[[大垣城]]内に移転。[[戸田氏]]は当神社の他、[[熊野権現|熊野神社]]など7社を大垣城内に祀っていた。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）4月頃、大垣城下の本町の住民が旧藩主の[[戸田氏共]]に願い出、大垣城内の貴船神社と熊野神社を本町の鎮守として祀る。同年10月、遮那院（[[白鳳]]年間に[[百済]]の王子沙門金珠が創建したという寺。明治2年廃寺。現在の大垣市清水町付近）の東惣門櫓台跡地に貴船神社と熊野神社を合祀、貴船神社と称した。 == 交通機関 == *[[東海道本線]]・[[養老鉄道]]・[[樽見鉄道]][[大垣駅]]より徒歩10分。 == その他 == *水門川沿いに水門川遊歩道四季の路が整備されている。隣接する貴船広場は、様々な[[イベント]]の会場となる他、[[イルミネーション]]が施される。 *毎年11月3日に行われる「本町お火焚きまつり」では、本町通りが[[歩行者天国]]となり、[[フリーマーケット]]などが行われる。 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしや おおかきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:貴船神社]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]] [[Category:大垣藩]]

{{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=大垣市}} |所在地 = 岐阜県大垣市本町1丁目75 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[高龗神|高龗大神]]
[[家津御子命大神]] |社格 = |創建 = 江戸時代初め |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月5日 |神事 = 本町お火焚きまつり }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]本町にある[[神社]]。大垣市本町の[[水門川]]沿いにある。 == 祭神 == *[[高龗神|高龗大神]] *[[家津御子命大神]] == 由緒 == [[戸田氏鉄]]が[[摂津国]][[尼崎藩]]藩主の時、[[京都]]の[[貴船神社]]から分社して祀ったのが始まりという。戸田氏鉄が尼崎藩藩主であったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）から[[寛永]]12年（[[1635年]]）であることから、分社したのはこの時期と推測される。 [[寛永]]12年（1635年）に[[美濃国]][[大垣藩]]に移封された際、当神社も[[大垣城]]内に移転。[[戸田氏]]は当神社の他、[[熊野権現|熊野神社]]など7社を大垣城内に祀っていた。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）4月頃、大垣城下の本町の住民が旧藩主の[[戸田氏共]]に願い出、大垣城内の貴船神社と熊野神社を本町の鎮守として祀る。同年10月、遮那院（[[白鳳]]年間に[[百済]]の王子沙門金珠が創建したという寺。明治2年廃寺。現在の大垣市清水町付近）の東惣門櫓台跡地に貴船神社と熊野神社を合祀、貴船神社と称した。 == 交通機関 == *[[東海道本線]]・[[養老鉄道]]・[[樽見鉄道]][[大垣駅]]より徒歩10分。 == その他 == *水門川沿いに水門川遊歩道四季の路が整備されている。隣接する貴船広場は、様々な[[イベント]]の会場となる他、[[イルミネーション]]が施される。 *毎年11月3日に行われる「本町お火焚きまつり」では、本町通りが[[歩行者天国]]となり、[[フリーマーケット]]などが行われる。 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしや おおかきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:貴船神社]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]] [[Category:大垣藩]]

{{出典の明記| date = 2024年5月}} {{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像=[[File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 岐阜県大垣市本町1丁目75 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[高龗神|高龗大神]]
[[家津御子命大神]] |社格 = |創建 = 江戸時代初め |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月5日 |神事 = 本町お火焚きまつり }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]本町にある[[神社]]。大垣市本町の[[水門川]]沿いにある。 == 祭神 == *[[高龗神|高龗大神]] *[[家津御子命大神]] == 由緒 == [[戸田氏鉄]]が[[摂津国]][[尼崎藩]]藩主の時、[[京都]]の[[貴船神社]]から分社して祀ったのが始まりという。戸田氏鉄が尼崎藩藩主であったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）から[[寛永]]12年（[[1635年]]）であることから、分社したのはこの時期と推測される。 [[寛永]]12年（1635年）に[[美濃国]][[大垣藩]]に移封された際、当神社も[[大垣城]]内に移転。[[戸田氏]]は当神社の他、[[熊野権現|熊野神社]]など7社を大垣城内に祀っていた。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）4月頃、大垣城下の本町の住民が旧藩主の[[戸田氏共]]に願い出、大垣城内の貴船神社と熊野神社を本町の鎮守として祀る。同年10月、遮那院（[[白鳳]]年間に[[百済]]の王子沙門金珠が創建したという寺。明治2年廃寺。現在の大垣市清水町付近）の東惣門櫓台跡地に貴船神社と熊野神社を合祀、貴船神社と称した。 == 交通機関 == *[[東海道本線]]・[[養老鉄道]]・[[樽見鉄道]][[大垣駅]]より徒歩10分。 == その他 == *水門川沿いに水門川遊歩道四季の路が整備されている。隣接する貴船広場は、様々な[[イベント]]の会場となる他、[[イルミネーション]]が施される。 *毎年11月3日に行われる「本町お火焚きまつり」では、本町通りが[[歩行者天国]]となり、[[フリーマーケット]]などが行われる。 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしや おおかきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:貴船神社]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大垣市の建築物]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attriubtion, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]]

Later on the categories should be renamed too.]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]]

[[家津御子命大神]] |社格 = |創建 = 江戸時代初め |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月5日 |神事 = 本町お火焚きまつり }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]本町にある[[神社]]。大垣市本町の[[水門川]]沿いにある。 == 祭神 == *[[高龗神|高龗大神]] *[[家津御子命大神]] == 由緒 == [[戸田氏鉄]]が[[摂津国]][[尼崎藩]]藩主の時、[[京都]]の[[貴船神社]]から分社して祀ったのが始まりという。戸田氏鉄が尼崎藩藩主であったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）から[[寛永]]12年（[[1635年]]）であることから、分社したのはこの時期と推測される。 [[寛永]]12年（1635年）に[[美濃国]][[大垣藩]]に移封された際、当神社も[[大垣城]]内に移転。[[戸田氏]]は当神社の他、[[熊野権現|熊野神社]]など7社を大垣城内に祀っていた。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）4月頃、大垣城下の本町の住民が旧藩主の[[戸田氏共]]に願い出、大垣城内の貴船神社と熊野神社を本町の鎮守として祀る。同年10月、遮那院（[[白鳳]]年間に[[百済]]の王子沙門金珠が創建したという寺。明治2年廃寺。現在の大垣市清水町付近）の東惣門櫓台跡地に貴船神社と熊野神社を合祀、貴船神社と称した。 == 交通機関 == *[[東海道本線]]・[[養老鉄道]]・[[樽見鉄道]][[大垣駅]]より徒歩10分。 == その他 == *水門川沿いに水門川遊歩道四季の路が整備されている。隣接する貴船広場は、様々な[[イベント]]の会場となる他、[[イルミネーション]]が施される。 *毎年11月3日に行われる「本町お火焚きまつり」では、本町通りが[[歩行者天国]]となり、[[フリーマーケット]]などが行われる。 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしや おおかきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:貴船神社]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大垣市の建築物]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記| date = 2024年5月}} {{神社 |name = 貴船神社 |image=[[File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = 岐阜県大垣市本町1丁目75 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} |deity = [[高龗神|高龗大神]]
[[家津御子命大神]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = 江戸時代初め |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = 8月5日 |神事 = 本町お火焚きまつり }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]本町にある[[神社]]。大垣市本町の[[水門川]]沿いにある。 == deity == *[[高龗神|高龗大神]] *[[家津御子命大神]] == 由緒 == [[戸田氏鉄]]が[[摂津国]][[尼崎藩]]藩主の時、[[京都]]の[[貴船神社]]から分社して祀ったのが始まりという。戸田氏鉄が尼崎藩藩主であったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）から[[寛永]]12年（[[1635年]]）であることから、分社したのはこの時期と推測される。 [[寛永]]12年（1635年）に[[美濃国]][[大垣藩]]に移封された際、当神社も[[大垣城]]内に移転。[[戸田氏]]は当神社の他、[[熊野権現|熊野神社]]など7社を大垣城内に祀っていた。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）4月頃、大垣城下の本町の住民が旧藩主の[[戸田氏共]]に願い出、大垣城内の貴船神社と熊野神社を本町の鎮守として祀る。同年10月、遮那院（[[白鳳]]年間に[[百済]]の王子沙門金珠が創建したという寺。明治2年廃寺。現在の大垣市清水町付近）の東惣門櫓台跡地に貴船神社と熊野神社を合祀、貴船神社と称した。 == 交通機関 == *[[東海道本線]]・[[養老鉄道]]・[[樽見鉄道]][[大垣駅]]より徒歩10分。 == その他 == *水門川沿いに水門川遊歩道四季の路が整備されている。隣接する貴船広場は、様々な[[イベント]]の会場となる他、[[イルミネーション]]が施される。 *毎年11月3日に行われる「本町お火焚きまつり」では、本町通りが[[歩行者天国]]となり、[[フリーマーケット]]などが行われる。 {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed:きふねしんしや おおかきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大垣市の建築物]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

[[家津御子命大神]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = 江戸時代初め |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = 8月5日 |神事 = 本町お火焚きまつり }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]本町にある[[神社]]。大垣市本町の[[水門川]]沿いにある。 == deity == *[[高龗神|高龗大神]] *[[家津御子命大神]] == 由緒 == [[戸田氏鉄]]が[[摂津国]][[尼崎藩]]藩主の時、[[京都]]の[[貴船神社]]から分社して祀ったのが始まりという。戸田氏鉄が尼崎藩藩主であったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616]]）から[[寛永]]12年（[[1635]]）であることから、分社したのはこの時期と推測される。 [[寛永]]12年（1635年）に[[美濃国]][[大垣藩]]に移封された際、当神社も[[大垣城]]内に移転。[[戸田氏]]は当神社の他、[[熊野権現|熊野神社]]など7社を大垣城内に祀っていた。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871]]）4月頃、大垣城下の本町の住民が旧藩主の[[戸田氏共]]に願い出、大垣城内の貴船神社と熊野神社を本町の鎮守として祀る。同年10月、遮那院（[[白鳳]]年間に[[百済]]の王子沙門金珠が創建したという寺。明治2年廃寺。現在の大垣市清水町付近）の東惣門櫓台跡地に貴船神社と熊野神社を合祀、貴船神社と称した。 == 交通機関 == *[[東海道本線]]・[[養老鉄道]]・[[樽見鉄道]][[大垣駅]]より徒歩10分。 == その他 == *水門川沿いに水門川遊歩道四季の路が整備されている。隣接する貴船広場は、様々な[[イベント]]の会場となる他、[[イルミネーション]]が施される。 *毎年11月3日に行われる「本町お火焚きまつり」では、本町通りが[[歩行者天国]]となり、[[フリーマーケット]]などが行われる。 {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|きふねしんしや おおかきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大垣市の建築物]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages goodbye $1]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]][[Category:qq]]

{{出典の明記| date = 2024年5月}} {{神社 |name = 貴船神社 |image=[[File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = 岐阜県大垣市本町1丁目75 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} |deity = [[高龗神|高龗大神]]
[[家津御子命大神]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = 江戸時代初め |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = 8月5日 |神事 = 本町お火焚きまつり }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]本町にある[[神社]]。大垣市本町の[[水門川]]沿いにある。 == deity == *[[高龗神|高龗大神]] *[[家津御子命大神]] == 由緒 == [[戸田氏鉄]]が[[摂津国]][[尼崎藩]]藩主の時、[[京都]]の[[貴船神社]]から分社して祀ったのが始まりという。戸田氏鉄が尼崎藩藩主であったのは[[Genna]]2年（[[1616]]）から[[寛永]]12年（[[1635]]）であることから、分社したのはこの時期と推測される。 [[寛永]]12年（1635年）に[[美濃国]][[大垣藩]]に移封された際、当神社も[[大垣城]]内に移転。[[戸田氏]]は当神社の他、[[熊野権現|熊野神社]]など7社を大垣城内に祀っていた。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871]]）4月頃、大垣城下の本町の住民が旧藩主の[[戸田氏共]]に願い出、大垣城内の貴船神社と熊野神社を本町の鎮守として祀る。同年10月、遮那院（{{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо}}年間に[[百済]]の王子沙門金珠が創建したという寺。明治2年廃寺。現在の大垣市清水町付近）の東惣門櫓台跡地に貴船神社と熊野神社を合祀、貴船神社と称した。 == 交通機関 == *[[Tōkaidō Main Line]]・[[養老鉄道]]・[[樽見鉄道]][[大垣駅]]より徒歩10分。 == その他 == *水門川沿いに水門川遊歩道四季の路が整備されている。隣接する貴船広場は、様々な[[イベント]]の会場となる他、[[イルミネーション]]が施される。 *毎年11月3日に行われる「本町お火焚きまつり」では、本町通りが[[歩行者天国]]となり、[[フリーマーケット]]などが行われる。 {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|きふねしんしや おおかきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大垣市の建築物]] [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]][[Category:11]]

{{Source citation| date = May 2024}} {{Shrine | name = Kifune Shrine | image = [[File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | location = 1-75, Honmachi, Ōgaki, Gifu Prefecture | coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates 2-level DMS|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} | deity = {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神}}
{{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | type = | Founded = Early Edo period | Main Shrine = | Alternate Name = | Associated Sites = | Festival = August 5 | Ritual = Honmachi Fire Burning Festival }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in Honmachi, {{ill|Ogaki City|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏鉄}}, while serving as the lord of the {{ill|Amagasaki Domain|ja|尼崎藩}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna}} 2 ({{ill|1616|ja|1616}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永}} 12 ({{ill|1635|ja|1635}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ogaki Domain|ja|大垣藩}} of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ogaki Castle|ja|大垣城}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治}} 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏共}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ogaki Station|ja|大垣駅}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|Kifune Shrine Ogaki City}} [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Ogaki Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[Category:11]] [[category:completion tier 1]] [[ja:貴船神社]]

The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|Kifune Shrine Ogaki City}} [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Ogaki Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[Category:11]] [[category:completion tier 1]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[Category:New]]

In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=draft}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|12=draft}} 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏共|12=draft}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=draft}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|12=draft}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ogaki Station|ja|大垣駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=draft}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=draft}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=draft}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=draft}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=draft}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|Kifune Shrine Ogaki City}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Ogaki Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]]

revision.text:
{{Source citation| date = May 2024}} {{Shrine | name = Kifune Shrine | image = [[File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | location = 1-75, Honmachi, Ōgaki, Gifu Prefecture | coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates 2-level DMS|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} | deity = {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | type = | Founded = Early Edo period | Main Shrine = | Alternate Name = | Associated Sites = | Festival = August 5 | Ritual = Honmachi Fire Burning Festival }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Honmachi, {{ill|Ogaki City|ja|大垣市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}}. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|12=draft}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=draft}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏鉄|12=draft}}, while serving as the lord of the {{ill|Amagasaki Domain|ja|尼崎藩|12=draft}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}, enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=draft}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1616|ja|1616|12=draft}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1635|ja|1635|12=draft}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ogaki Domain|ja|大垣藩|12=draft}} of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|12=draft}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ogaki Castle|ja|大垣城|12=draft}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=draft}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|12=draft}} 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏共|12=draft}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=draft}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|12=draft}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ogaki Station|ja|大垣駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=draft}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=draft}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=draft}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=draft}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=draft}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|Kifune Shrine Ogaki City}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Ogaki Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]]

11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:貴船神社 (大垣市) up to rev 100444370
revision.text:
{{出典の明記| date = 2024年5月}} {{神社 |名称 = 貴船神社 |画像=[[File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 岐阜県大垣市本町1丁目75 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[高龗神|高龗大神]]
[[家津御子命大神]] |社格 = |創建 = 江戸時代初め |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月5日 |神事 = 本町お火焚きまつり }} '''貴船神社'''（きふねじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]本町にある[[神社]]。大垣市本町の[[水門川]]沿いにある。 == 祭神 == *[[高龗神|高龗大神]] *[[家津御子命大神]] == 由緒 == [[戸田氏鉄]]が[[摂津国]][[尼崎藩]]藩主の時、[[京都]]の[[貴船神社]]から分社して祀ったのが始まりという。戸田氏鉄が尼崎藩藩主であったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）から[[寛永]]12年（[[1635年]]）であることから、分社したのはこの時期と推測される。 [[寛永]]12年（1635年）に[[美濃国]][[大垣藩]]に移封された際、当神社も[[大垣城]]内に移転。[[戸田氏]]は当神社の他、[[熊野権現|熊野神社]]など7社を大垣城内に祀っていた。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）4月頃、大垣城下の本町の住民が旧藩主の[[戸田氏共]]に願い出、大垣城内の貴船神社と熊野神社を本町の鎮守として祀る。同年10月、遮那院（[[白鳳]]年間に[[百済]]の王子沙門金珠が創建したという寺。明治2年廃寺。現在の大垣市清水町付近）の東惣門櫓台跡地に貴船神社と熊野神社を合祀、貴船神社と称した。 == 交通機関 == *[[東海道本線]]・[[養老鉄道]]・[[樽見鉄道]][[大垣駅]]より徒歩10分。 == その他 == *水門川沿いに水門川遊歩道四季の路が整備されている。隣接する貴船広場は、様々な[[イベント]]の会場となる他、[[イルミネーション]]が施される。 *毎年11月3日に行われる「本町お火焚きまつり」では、本町通りが[[歩行者天国]]となり、[[フリーマーケット]]などが行われる。 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしや おおかきし}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:貴船神社]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大垣市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Source citation| date = May 2024}} {{Shrine | name = Kifune Shrine | image = [[File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = 1-75, Honmachi, Ōgaki, Gifu Prefecture | coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates 2-level DMS|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} | deity = {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=draft|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = | Founded = Early Edo period | Main Shrine = | Alternate Name = | Associated Sites = | Festival = August 5 | Ritual = Honmachi Fire Burning Festival }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Honmachi, {{ill|Ogaki City|ja|大垣市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|12=draft|12=draft}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏鉄|12=draft|12=draft}}, while serving as the lord of the {{ill|Amagasaki Domain|ja|尼崎藩|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft|12=draft}}, enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1616|ja|1616|12=draft|12=draft}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1635|ja|1635|12=draft|12=draft}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ogaki Domain|ja|大垣藩|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ogaki Castle|ja|大垣城|12=draft|12=draft}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft|12=draft}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏共|12=draft|12=draft}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=draft|12=draft}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ogaki Station|ja|大垣駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=draft|12=draft}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=draft|12=draft}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=draft|12=draft}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|Kifune Shrine Ogaki City}} {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Ogaki Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]]

It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while serving as the lord of the {{ill|Amagasaki Domain|ja|尼崎藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1616|ja|1616|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1635|ja|1635|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ogaki Domain|ja|大垣藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ogaki Castle|ja|大垣城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏共|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ogaki Station|ja|大垣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Ogaki Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]]

In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ogaki Station|ja|大垣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Ogaki Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

{{Source citation| date = May 2024}} {{Shrine | name = Kifune Shrine | image = [[File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = 1-75, Honmachi, Ōgaki, Gifu Prefecture | coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates 2-level DMS|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} | deity = {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = | Founded = Early Edo period | Main Shrine = | Alternate Name = | Associated Sites = | Festival = August 5 | Ritual = Honmachi Fire Burning Festival }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Honmachi, {{ill|Ogaki City|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while serving as the lord of the {{ill|Amagasaki Domain|ja|尼崎藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1616|ja|1616|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1635|ja|1635|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ogaki Domain|ja|大垣藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ogaki Castle|ja|大垣城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏共|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ogaki Station|ja|大垣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Ogaki Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|qq=}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ogaki Station|ja|大垣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaki Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Ogaki Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

{{Source citation| date = May 2024}} {{Shrine | name = Kifune Shrine | image = [[File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | location = 1-75, Honmachi, Ōgaki, Gifu Prefecture | coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates 2-level DMS|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} | deity = {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|qq=}}
{{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | type = | Founded = Early Edo period | Main Shrine = | Alternate Name = | Associated Sites = | Festival = August 5 | Ritual = Honmachi Fire Burning Festival }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine|qq=}} located in Honmachi, {{ill|Ogaki City|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|qq=}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Shitetsu|qq=}}, while serving as the lord of the {{ill|Amagasaki Domain|ja|尼崎藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki Domain|qq=}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Province|qq=}}, enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1616|ja|1616|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1616|qq=}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei Era|qq=}} 12 ({{ill|1635|ja|1635|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1635|qq=}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei Era|qq=}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ogaki Domain|ja|大垣藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaki Domain|qq=}} of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ogaki Castle|ja|大垣城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaki Castle|qq=}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|qq=}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏共|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Shitetsu|qq=}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ogaki Station|ja|大垣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaki Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=}}. {{Shinto stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Ogaki Domain]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ogaki Station|ja|大垣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaki Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=}}. {{Shinto stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]]

{{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | type = | Founded = Early Edo period | Main Shrine = | Alternate Name = | Associated Sites = | Festival = August 5 | Ritual = Honmachi Fire Burning Festival }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine|qq=}} located in Honmachi, {{ill|Ogaki City|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|qq=}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Shitetsu|qq=}}, while serving as the lord of the {{ill|Amagasaki Domain|ja|尼崎藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki Domain|qq=}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Province|qq=}}, enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1616|ja|1616|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1616|qq=}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei Era|qq=}} 12 ({{ill|1635|ja|1635|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1635|qq=}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei Era|qq=}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ogaki Domain|ja|大垣藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaki Domain|qq=}} of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ogaki Castle|ja|大垣城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaki Castle|qq=}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|qq=}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Shitetsu|ja|戸田氏共|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Shitetsu|qq=}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ogaki Station|ja|大垣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaki Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|qq=}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when [[:en:Toda Ujikane|Toda Shitetsu]], while serving as the lord of the [[:en:Amagasaki Domain|Amagasaki Domain]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1616|ja|1616|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1616|qq=}}) to [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 ({{ill|1635|ja|1635|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1635|qq=}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the [[:en:Ōgaki Domain|Ogaki Domain]] of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], it was relocated within [[:en:Ōgaki Castle|Ogaki Castle]]. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|qq=}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=}}. {{Shinto stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]] [[Category:大垣市の建築物]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Source citation| date = May 2024}} {{Shrine | name = Kifune Shrine | image = [[File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = 1-75, Honmachi, Ōgaki, Gifu Prefecture | coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates 2-level DMS|35|21|46.9|N|136|37|10.5|E|scale:25000}} | deity = {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|qq=}}
{{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = | Founded = Early Edo period | Main Shrine = | Alternate Name = | Associated Sites = | Festival = August 5 | Ritual = Honmachi Fire Burning Festival }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[:en:Ōgaki|Ogaki City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|qq=}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when [[:en:Toda Ujikane|Toda Shitetsu]], while serving as the lord of the [[:en:Amagasaki Domain|Amagasaki Domain]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1616|ja|1616|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1616|qq=}}) to [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 ({{ill|1635|ja|1635|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1635|qq=}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the [[:en:Ōgaki Domain|Ogaki Domain]] of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], it was relocated within [[:en:Ōgaki Castle|Ogaki Castle]]. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|qq=}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=}}. {{Shinto stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:History of Ogaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki]] [[Category:History of Ogaki]] [[Category:大垣藩]]

Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1000}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市}}. {{Shinto stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki]] [[Category:History of Ogaki]] [[Category:大垣藩]]

{{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = | Founded = Early Edo period | Main Shrine = | Alternate Name = | Associated Sites = | Festival = August 5 | Ritual = Honmachi Fire Burning Festival }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[:en:Ōgaki|Ogaki City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|qq=}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when [[:en:Toda Ujikane|Toda Shitetsu]], while serving as the lord of the [[:en:Amagasaki Domain|Amagasaki Domain]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ([[:en:1616|1616]]) to [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 ({{ill|1635|ja|1635|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1635|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the [[:en:Ōgaki Domain|Ogaki Domain]] of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], it was relocated within [[:en:Ōgaki Castle|Ogaki Castle]]. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|qq=}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1000}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市}}. {{Shinto stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki]] [[Category:History of Ogaki]] [[Category:大垣藩]]

In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|qq=}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki]] [[Category:History of Ogaki]] [[Category:大垣藩]]

It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|qq=}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when [[:en:Toda Ujikane|Toda Shitetsu]], while serving as the lord of the [[:en:Amagasaki Domain|Amagasaki Domain]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ([[:en:1616|1616]]) to [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 ([[:en:1635|1635]]), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the [[:en:Ōgaki Domain|Ogaki Domain]] of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], it was relocated within [[:en:Ōgaki Castle|Ogaki Castle]]. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|qq=}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki]] [[Category:History of Ogaki]] [[Category:Ōgaki Domain]]

It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|qq=}} in Honmachi. == deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == It is said that the origin dates back to when [[:en:Toda Ujikane|Toda Shitetsu]], while serving as the lord of the [[:en:Amagasaki Domain|Amagasaki Domain]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ([[:en:1616|1616]]) to [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 ([[:en:1635|1635]]), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period. In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the [[:en:Ōgaki Domain|Ogaki Domain]] of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], it was relocated within [[:en:Ōgaki Castle|Ogaki Castle]]. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|qq=}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle. Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine. == Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}}. == Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市}}. {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (Ogaki City)]] [[ja:貴船神社]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (大垣市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki]] [[Category:History of Ogaki]] [[Category:Ōgaki Domain]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]
