'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[:en:Ōgaki|Ogaki City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|qq=|1=Suimon River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549495}} in Honmachi.
== deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神|1=Kuraokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q644201}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == It is said that the origin dates back to when [[:en:Toda Ujikane|Toda Shitetsu]], while serving as the lord of the [[:en:Amagasaki Domain|Amagasaki Domain]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ([[:en:1616|1616]]) to [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 ([[:en:1635|1635]]), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the [[:en:Ōgaki Domain|Ogaki Domain]] of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], it was relocated within [[:en:Ōgaki Castle|Ogaki Castle]]. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|qq=|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=|1=hokōsha tengoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.

Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ([[:en:1616|1616]]) to [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 ([[:en:1635|1635]]), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.

In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|qq=|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|qq=|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|qq=|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|qq=|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|qq=|1=hokōsha tengoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.
{{Shinto stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634810]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634810]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Source citation| date = May 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kifune Shrine | image = File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = 1-75, Honmachi, Ōgaki, Gifu Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|35.363028|N|136.619583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = | Founded = Early Edo period | Main Shrine = | Alternate Name = | Associated Sites = | Festival = August 5 | Ritual = Honmachi Fire Burning Festival | location = {{ill|Ogaki|WD=Q384926}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[:en:Ōgaki|Ogaki City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|1=Suimon River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549495}} in Honmachi.
== deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神|1=Kuraokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q644201}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == It is said that the origin dates back to when [[:en:Toda Ujikane|Toda Shitetsu]], while serving as the lord of the [[:en:Amagasaki Domain|Amagasaki Domain]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ([[:en:1616|1616]]) to [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 ([[:en:1635|1635]]), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the [[:en:Ōgaki Domain|Ogaki Domain]] of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], it was relocated within [[:en:Ōgaki Castle|Ogaki Castle]]. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=hokōsha tengoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.
{{Shinto stub}}
{{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (大垣市)|version=100444370|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=hokōsha tengoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634810]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634810]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Source citation| date = May 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kifune Shrine | image = File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = 1-75, Honmachi, Ōgaki, Gifu Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|35.363028|N|136.619583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = | Founded = Early Edo period | Main Shrine = | Alternate Name = | Associated Sites = | Festival = August 5 | Ritual = Honmachi Fire Burning Festival | location = {{ill|Ogaki|WD=Q384926}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[:en:Ōgaki|Ogaki City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|1=Suimon River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549495}} in Honmachi.
== deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神|1=Kuraokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q644201}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == It is said that the origin dates back to when [[:en:Toda Ujikane|Toda Shitetsu]], while serving as the lord of the [[:en:Amagasaki Domain|Amagasaki Domain]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ([[:en:1616|1616]]) to [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 ([[:en:1635|1635]]), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the [[:en:Ōgaki Domain|Ogaki Domain]] of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], it was relocated within [[:en:Ōgaki Castle|Ogaki Castle]]. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=hokōsha tengoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.
{{Shinto stub}}

== History == It is said that the origin dates back to when [[:en:Toda Ujikane|Toda Shitetsu]], while serving as the lord of the [[:en:Amagasaki Domain|Amagasaki Domain]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ([[:en:1616|1616]]) to [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 ([[:en:1635|1635]]), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the [[:en:Ōgaki Domain|Ogaki Domain]] of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], it was relocated within [[:en:Ōgaki Castle|Ogaki Castle]]. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=hokōsha tengoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.

The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=hokōsha tengoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634810]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634810]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Source citation| date = May 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kifune Shrine | image = File:Kifune-jinja shrine 20160618-01.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = 1-75, Honmachi, Ōgaki, Gifu Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|35.363028|N|136.619583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = | Founded = Early Edo period | Main Shrine = | Alternate Name = | Associated Sites = | Festival = August 5 | Ritual = Honmachi Fire Burning Festival | location = {{ill|Ogaki|WD=Q384926}} }}
'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[:en:Ōgaki|Ogaki City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|1=Suimon River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549495}} in Honmachi.
== deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神|1=Kuraokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q644201}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == It is said that the origin dates back to when [[:en:Toda Ujikane|Toda Shitetsu]], while serving as the lord of the [[:en:Amagasaki Domain|Amagasaki Domain]] in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ([[:en:1616|1616]]) to [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 ([[:en:1635|1635]]), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the [[:en:Ōgaki Domain|Ogaki Domain]] of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], it was relocated within [[:en:Ōgaki Castle|Ogaki Castle]]. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 4 ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, [[:en:Toda Ujitaka|Toda Shitetsu]], to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Ōgaki Station|Ogaki Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=hokōsha tengoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.
{{Shinto stub}}

Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ({{ill|1616|en|1616|WD=Q6788|lt=1616}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era}} 12 ({{ill|1635|en|1635|WD=Q6879|lt=1635}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.

In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Ujitaka|en|Toda Ujitaka|WD=Q7398863|lt=Toda Shitetsu}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in Honmachi, {{ill|Ōgaki|en|Ōgaki|WD=Q384926|lt=Ogaki City}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|1=Suimon River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549495}} in Honmachi.
== deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神|1=Kuraokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q644201}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == It is said that the origin dates back to when {{ill|Toda Ujikane|en|Toda Ujikane|WD=Q3530228|lt=Toda Shitetsu}}, while serving as the lord of the {{ill|Amagasaki Domain|en|Amagasaki Domain|WD=Q903474|lt=Amagasaki Domain}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}, enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ({{ill|1616|en|1616|WD=Q6788|lt=1616}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era}} 12 ({{ill|1635|en|1635|WD=Q6879|lt=1635}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.
In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ōgaki Domain|en|Ōgaki Domain|WD=Q839430|lt=Ogaki Domain}} of {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ōgaki Castle|en|Ōgaki Castle|WD=Q1067359|lt=Ogaki Castle}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Ujitaka|en|Toda Ujitaka|WD=Q7398863|lt=Toda Shitetsu}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ōgaki Station|en|Ōgaki Station|WD=Q871579|lt=Ogaki Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=hokōsha tengoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.
{{Shinto stub}}

[[ja:貴船神社 (大垣市)]]
[[ja:貴船神社]]

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in Honmachi, {{ill|Ōgaki|en|Ōgaki|WD=Q384926|lt=Ogaki City}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|1=Suimon River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549495}} in Honmachi.
== deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神|1=Kuraokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q644201}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ({{ill|1616|en|1616|WD=Q6788|lt=1616}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era}} 12 ({{ill|1635|en|1635|WD=Q6879|lt=1635}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.
In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ōgaki Domain|en|Ōgaki Domain|WD=Q839430|lt=Ogaki Domain}} of {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ōgaki Castle|en|Ōgaki Castle|WD=Q1067359|lt=Ogaki Castle}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Ujitaka|en|Toda Ujitaka|WD=Q7398863|lt=Toda Shitetsu}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ōgaki Station|en|Ōgaki Station|WD=Q871579|lt=Ogaki Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=hokōsha tengoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.

== deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神|1=Kuraokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q644201}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == It is said that the origin dates back to when {{ill|Toda Ujikane|en|Toda Ujikane|WD=Q3530228|lt=Toda Shitetsu}}, while serving as the lord of the {{ill|Amagasaki Domain|en|Amagasaki Domain|WD=Q903474|lt=Amagasaki Domain}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}, enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ({{ill|1616|en|1616|WD=Q6788|lt=1616}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era}} 12 ({{ill|1635|en|1635|WD=Q6879|lt=1635}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.
In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ōgaki Domain|en|Ōgaki Domain|WD=Q839430|lt=Ogaki Domain}} of {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ōgaki Castle|en|Ōgaki Castle|WD=Q1067359|lt=Ogaki Castle}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Ujitaka|en|Toda Ujitaka|WD=Q7398863|lt=Toda Shitetsu}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana‑in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Ōgaki Station|en|Ōgaki Station|WD=Q871579|lt=Ogaki Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=hokōsha tengoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.
{{Shinto stub}}

[[ja:貴船神社]]

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in Honmachi, {{ill|Ōgaki|en|Ōgaki|WD=Q384926|lt=Ogaki City}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|1=Suimon River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549495}} in Honmachi.
== deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神|1=Kuraokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q644201}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ({{ill|1616|en|1616|WD=Q6788|lt=1616}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era}} 12 ({{ill|1635|en|1635|WD=Q6879|lt=1635}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.
In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ōgaki Domain|en|Ōgaki Domain|WD=Q839430|lt=Ogaki Domain}} of {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ōgaki Castle|en|Ōgaki Castle|WD=Q1067359|lt=Ogaki Castle}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Ujitaka|en|Toda Ujitaka|WD=Q7398863|lt=Toda Shitetsu}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana-in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgaki Station|en|Ōgaki Station|WD=Q871579|lt=Ogaki Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.

Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ({{ill|1616|en|1616|WD=Q6788|lt=1616|1=1616}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era|1=Kan'ei}} 12 ({{ill|1635|en|1635|WD=Q6879|lt=1635|1=1635}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.
In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era|1=Kan'ei}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ōgaki Domain|en|Ōgaki Domain|WD=Q839430|lt=Ogaki Domain|1=Ōgaki Domain}} of {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ōgaki Castle|en|Ōgaki Castle|WD=Q1067359|lt=Ogaki Castle|1=Ōgaki Castle}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Ujitaka|en|Toda Ujitaka|WD=Q7398863|lt=Toda Shitetsu|1=Toda Ujitaka}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana-in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgaki Station|en|Ōgaki Station|WD=Q871579|lt=Ogaki Station|1=Ōgaki Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=car-free street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=Flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.

'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Honmachi, {{ill|Ōgaki|en|Ōgaki|WD=Q384926|lt=Ogaki City|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is situated along the {{ill|Minamonogawa|ja|水門川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamonogawa|1=Suimon River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549495}} in Honmachi.
== deity == * {{ill|Takamori no Kami|ja|高龗神|lt=Takamori Ōkami|lt_ja=高龗大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神|1=Kuraokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q644201}} * {{ill|Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja|家津御子命大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsu-no-miko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == It is said that the origin dates back to when {{ill|Toda Ujikane|en|Toda Ujikane|WD=Q3530228|lt=Toda Shitetsu|1=Toda Ujikane}}, while serving as the lord of the {{ill|Amagasaki Domain|en|Amagasaki Domain|WD=Q903474|lt=Amagasaki Domain|1=Amagasaki Domain}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}, enshrined a branch shrine from the Kifune Shrine of {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}. Since Toda Shitetsu held lordship from {{ill|Genna Era|ja|Genna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna Era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 ({{ill|1616|en|1616|WD=Q6788|lt=1616|1=1616}}) to {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era|1=Kan'ei}} 12 ({{ill|1635|en|1635|WD=Q6879|lt=1635|1=1635}}), it is presumed that the branch was established during that period.
In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era|1=Kan'ei}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ōgaki Domain|en|Ōgaki Domain|WD=Q839430|lt=Ogaki Domain|1=Ōgaki Domain}} of {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ōgaki Castle|en|Ōgaki Castle|WD=Q1067359|lt=Ogaki Castle|1=Ōgaki Castle}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Ujitaka|en|Toda Ujitaka|WD=Q7398863|lt=Toda Shitetsu|1=Toda Ujitaka}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana-in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgaki Station|en|Ōgaki Station|WD=Q871579|lt=Ogaki Station|1=Ōgaki Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=car-free street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=Flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385870}}.

In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era|1=Kan'ei}} 12 (1635), when the shrine was transferred to the {{ill|Ōgaki Domain|en|Ōgaki Domain|qid=Q839430|lt=Ogaki Domain|1=Ōgaki Domain}} of {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, it was relocated within {{ill|Ōgaki Castle|en|Ōgaki Castle|qid=Q1067359|lt=Ogaki Castle|1=Ōgaki Castle}}. In addition to this shrine, the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11496536}} enshrined seven shrines—including {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野権現|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}—inside Ogaki Castle.
Around April of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|1871|en|1871|qid=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}}), the residents of Honmachi beneath Ogaki Castle petitioned the former lord, {{ill|Toda Ujitaka|en|Toda Ujitaka|qid=Q7398863|lt=Toda Shitetsu|1=Toda Ujitaka}}, to enshrine the Kifune Shrine and Kumano Shrine as the guardian shrines of Honmachi. In October of the same year, at the site of the former Higashisōmon Yagura on the grounds of Shana-in (a temple said to have been founded during the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580612}} period by the Baekje prince monk Geumju—and abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2—located in the vicinity of Shimizu-cho, Ogaki City), the two shrines were merged and enshrined under the name Kifune Shrine.
== Transportation == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgaki Station|en|Ōgaki Station|qid=Q871579|lt=Ogaki Station|1=Ōgaki Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|Tōkaidō Main Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoro Railway|ja|養老鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro Railway|1=Yoro Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666892}}, and {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11543718}}.
== Others == * Along the Minamonogawa, the Minamonogawa Promenade “Shiki no Michi” has been developed. The adjacent Kifune Plaza serves as a venue for various {{ill|Events|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Events|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1656682}} and is adorned with {{ill|Illumination|ja|イルミネーション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Illumination|1=illuminations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1658880}}. * During the annual “Honmachi Fire Burning Festival” held every November 3, Honmachi Street becomes a {{ill|Pedestrian Paradise|ja|歩行者天国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pedestrian Paradise|1=car-free street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48743705}}, hosting events such as a {{ill|Flea Market|ja|フリーマーケット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flea Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蚤の市|1=Flea market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q385870}}.
